출애굽기 18:1.content: Now Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses’ father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people, how Yahweh had brought Israel out of Egypt.

출애굽기 18:2.content: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, received Zipporah, Moses’ wife, after he had sent her away,

출애굽기 18:3.content: and her two sons. The name of one son was Gershom, for Moses said, “I have lived as a foreigner in a foreign land”.

출애굽기 18:4.content: The name of the other was Eliezer, for he said, “My father’s God was my help and delivered me from Pharaoh’s sword.”

출애굽기 18:5.content: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, came with Moses’ sons and his wife to Moses into the wilderness where he was encamped, at the Mountain of God.

출애굽기 18:6.content: He said to Moses, “I, your father-in-law Jethro, have come to you with your wife, and her two sons with her.”

출애굽기 18:7.content: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and bowed and kissed him. They asked each other of their welfare, and they came into the tent.

출애굽기 18:8.content: Moses told his father-in-law all that Yahweh had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the hardships that had come on them on the way, and how Yahweh delivered them.

출애굽기 18:9.content: Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which Yahweh had done to Israel, in that he had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.

출애굽기 18:10.content: Jethro said, “Blessed be Yahweh, who has delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh; who has delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.

출애굽기 18:11.content: Now I know that Yahweh is greater than all gods because of the way that they treated people arrogantly.”

출애굽기 18:12.content: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God. Aaron came with all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’ father-in-law before God.

출애굽기 18:13.content: On the next day, Moses sat to judge the people, and the people stood around Moses from the morning to the evening.

출애굽기 18:14.content: When Moses’ father-in-law saw all that he did to the people, he said, “What is this thing that you do for the people? Why do you sit alone, and all the people stand around you from morning to evening?”

출애굽기 18:15.content: Moses said to his father-in-law, “Because the people come to me to inquire of God.

출애굽기 18:16.content: When they have a matter, they come to me, and I judge between a man and his neighbor, and I make them know the statutes of God, and his laws.”

출애굽기 18:17.content: Moses’ father-in-law said to him, “The thing that you do is not good.

출애굽기 18:18.content: You will surely wear away, both you, and this people that is with you; for the thing is too heavy for you. You are not able to perform it yourself alone.

출애굽기 18:19.content: Listen now to my voice. I will give you counsel, and God be with you. You represent the people before God, and bring the causes to God.

출애굽기 18:20.content: You shall teach them the statutes and the laws, and shall show them the way in which they must walk, and the work that they must do.

출애굽기 18:21.content: Moreover you shall provide out of all the people able men which fear God: men of truth, hating unjust gain; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

출애굽기 18:22.content: Let them judge the people at all times. It shall be that every great matter they shall bring to you, but every small matter they shall judge themselves. So shall it be easier for you, and they shall share the load with you.

출애굽기 18:23.content: If you will do this thing, and God commands you so, then you will be able to endure, and all these people also will go to their place in peace.”

출애굽기 18:24.content: So Moses listened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said.

출애굽기 18:25.content: Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

출애굽기 18:26.content: They judged the people at all times. They brought the hard cases to Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.

출애굽기 18:27.content: Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went his way into his own land.
